data.title: Ethereum Stablecoin Supply Hits Record High of $180 Billion
data.description: Ethereum-based stablecoin supply has reached an unprecedented level, surpassing $180 billion, according to Token Terminal data. According to NS3.AI, Ethereum now represents approximately 60% of the stablecoin market, highlighting its dominant position in the sector. This milestone reflects the growing adoption and reliance on Ethereum's blockchain for stablecoin transactions and storage.

data.title: A senior Iranian source said the US is seeking to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: A senior Iranian source stated that the US seeks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but Iran will not open the strait for "empty promises." If the US attacks Iranian power plants, the entire region and Saudi Arabia will be plunged into complete darkness. Iran will also show flexibility when it sees the US demonstrate flexibility, and if the situation spirals out of control, its allies will close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait. (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams downplayed the Warsh nomination impasse: There is uncertainty regarding the confirmation timeline.
data.description: The timeline for Kevin Warsh's nomination as Federal Reserve Chair remains uncertain due to opposition from leading Republican senators to proceed with his nomination while incumbent Chairman Jerome Powell is still under investigation. New York Fed President and Vice Chairman of the Federal Reserve Policy Committee, Williams, stated that this uncertainty is not a problem for the Fed. The Federal Open Market Committee voted earlier this year to appoint Powell as chairman, and he can continue in that position until Warsh is confirmed. Williams said, "We're just focused on doing our job. There are no issues like continuity within the committee." (Jinshi)

data.title: Intel Partners with SpaceX, XAI, and Tesla for TeraFAB Project
data.description: Intel's stock rose by 1.6% in pre-market trading following the announcement of its collaboration with SpaceX, XAI, and Tesla on the TeraFAB project. According to Jin10, this initiative aims to advance technological innovations and enhance capabilities across various sectors. The partnership is expected to leverage the strengths of each company to drive progress in computing and space exploration. Intel's involvement in the TeraFAB project highlights its commitment to expanding its influence in cutting-edge technology fields.

data.title: Fed's Williams Projects GDP Growth Amid Stable Unemployment Rate
data.description: Federal Reserve official John Williams has indicated that policymakers can adopt a 'wait and see' approach for the time being. According to NS3.AI, Williams projects GDP growth for this year to be between 2% and 2.5%. He also anticipates that the unemployment rate will remain stable. Additionally, Williams commented on the complexity of the labor market and expects core inflation to decrease later this year.

data.title: International crude oil prices fluctuated upwards after Trump threatened Iran, while international gold and silver futures showed mixed results.
data.description: According to Gate data, international crude oil prices fluctuated upwards after Trump threatened Iran, saying "the entire civilization will perish." WTI crude oil rose 2.32% to $115.014 per barrel, while Brent crude oil rose 0.61% to $110.444 per barrel. International gold and silver futures were mixed, with COMEX gold futures rising 0.13% to $4691.0 per ounce, while COMEX silver futures fell 0.49% to $72.49 per ounce.

data.title: Apple Faces Potential Delays in Foldable iPhone Launch Due to Engineering Challenges
data.description: Apple is encountering engineering difficulties with its foldable iPhone models, which could result in shipment delays, according to Nikkei Asia. People familiar with the situation indicated that pilot tests did not meet expectations, potentially postponing mass production. According to NS3.AI, this development aligns with Apple CEO Tim Cook's sale of $16.5 million in Apple stock on April 2.

data.title: Bitdeer Unveils SEALMINER A4 Series with Enhanced Power Efficiency
data.description: Bitdeer has introduced the SEALMINER A4 series, featuring the flagship A4 Ultra Hydro model, which boasts a power efficiency of 9.45 J/TH. According to NS3.AI, the series comprises three models: the A4 Pro Air, A4 Pro Hydro, and A4 Ultra Hydro. Bitdeer reports that both the A4 Pro Air and A4 Pro Hydro achieve a power efficiency of 10.9 J/TH.

data.title: CME Group to Launch Avalanche and Sui Futures in 2026
data.description: CME Group has announced plans to introduce futures contracts for Avalanche (AVAX) and Sui (SUI) on May 4, 2026. According to Odaily, this move aims to expand its regulated cryptocurrency derivatives offerings, pending regulatory approval. Market participants will have the option to choose between micro and standard contracts. The AVAX futures will consist of 5,000 AVAX, while the micro AVAX futures will include 500 AVAX. Similarly, the SUI futures will comprise 50,000 SUI, with micro SUI futures containing 5,000 SUI.

data.title: Nobel laureate in physics warns: The window of opportunity for Bitcoin to face the threat of quantum computing is closing in.
data.description: John Martinis, former head of Google's quantum hardware and 2025 Nobel laureate in physics, warned that Bitcoin could become one of the first real-world targets of quantum computing attacks. He stated that recent Google research shows advanced quantum computers could derive Bitcoin's private keys in minutes, significantly lowering the computational security barriers currently relied upon by the network. Because Bitcoin relies on elliptic curve cryptography and its network upgrades are slow and decentralized, quantum threats are more difficult to counter than those of traditional financial systems. The public key exposure window during Bitcoin transaction broadcasts could be exploited by quantum computers to intercept funds before final confirmation. Martinis emphasized that while building such quantum computers remains an extremely challenging engineering task, the community cannot afford to be complacent and recommends planning quantum-resistant upgrades as early as possible, anticipating that related threats will gradually emerge over the next 5 to 10 years. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Robinhood launches ORCA
data.description: According to the official page, Robinhood has launched ORCA.

data.title: Coinbase Institutional: Maintains neutral view on the crypto market in Q2; BTC shows resilience.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Coinbase Institutional released its Q2 2026 crypto market outlook, maintaining a neutral stance. The report points out that the escalating situation in Iran disrupted market expectations for fiscal and monetary stimulus, driving funds back into cash assets. Despite significant global market volatility, Bitcoin's decline was significantly smaller than the S&P 500, demonstrating greater resilience amidst the fluctuations. The institution believes that the current macroeconomic environment is rapidly changing, with geopolitical risks intertwined with inflation and interest rate expectations, leaving the crypto market lacking a clear direction in the short term. Coinbase Institutional recommends a neutral allocation, focusing on highly liquid and well-consensus assets such as Bitcoin, awaiting trend signals after geopolitical risks ease and macroeconomic policies become clearer.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams: Powell to Chair FOMC Meeting Until New Chair Confirmed
data.description: Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams stated that Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will continue to preside over the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings until a new chair is confirmed. According to Jin10, this announcement comes amid ongoing discussions about the future leadership of the Federal Reserve. Williams emphasized the importance of continuity and stability in the central bank's operations during this transitional period. The confirmation process for the new chair is expected to proceed in the coming months, with Powell maintaining his role in the interim.

data.title: U.S. Economic Trends: Low Hiring, Low Layoffs, Low Unemployment
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has been overseeing an economy characterized by low hiring, low layoffs, and low unemployment, according to Jin10. Federal Reserve official Williams highlighted these trends, noting their impact on the overall economic landscape. The current situation reflects a stable job market, with fewer disruptions and a steady employment rate. This environment may influence future monetary policy decisions as the Federal Reserve continues to monitor economic indicators closely.

data.title: CME Group plans to launch AVAX and SUI futures contracts and is currently awaiting regulatory approval.
data.description: CME Group announced plans to launch Avalanche (AVAX) and Sui (SUI) futures contracts on May 4, 2026, further enriching its regulated cryptocurrency derivatives product line. The launch is currently awaiting regulatory approval. Market participants will be able to choose between micro or standard contracts: 1. AVAX Futures: 5,000 AVAX, Micro AVAX Futures: 500 AVAX; 2. SUI Futures: 50,000 SUI, Micro SUI Futures: 5,000 SUI.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams: A war with Iran would push up overall inflation; interest rates are currently at a perfectly appropriate level.
data.description: New York Fed President Williams stated that the impact of the Iran war will push up overall inflation, and the resulting inflationary factors will be directly reflected in overall inflation data. Taking energy factors into account, the inflation rate should be around 2.75%. The current focus is on overall inflation; core inflation has not changed significantly. Tariffs remain a significant factor in inflation, and overall inflation is expected to slow later this year. Monetary policy is currently in a favorable position, and a wait-and-see approach is advisable. Interest rates are currently at a perfectly appropriate level and can be adjusted if necessary. The labor market situation is quite complex, characterized by low hiring and low layoffs. (Jinshi)

data.title: DeFi Security Concerns Rise After $270 Million Drift Exploit
data.description: Drift has revealed that its recent $270 million exploit was the result of a six-month campaign involving fake identities, in-person meetings, and cultivated trust, rather than a flaw in smart contracts. According to NS3.AI, security leaders from ENS Labs, SVRN, Jupiter, dYdX Labs, and Jito Labs have emphasized the need for DeFi teams to consider contributors, multisigs, and operational security as critical attack surfaces. This incident highlights the importance of robust security measures in the decentralized finance sector to prevent similar breaches.

data.title: Polymarket's probability of a US-Iran ceasefire by May 15th has dropped to 37%, a 45% decrease in the past 24 hours.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, the probability of a ceasefire between the US and Iran by May 15th has dropped to 37% on Polymarket, a 45% decrease in 24 hours. The total trading volume of contracts related to the "US-Iran ceasefire" event has exceeded $107 million. The rules of this event contract state that if the US and Iran reach a formal ceasefire agreement (defined as a public announcement and mutual agreement to cease direct military action) by a specified date (11:59 PM Eastern Time), the market will mark it as "yes"; otherwise, it will be "no." A "formal ceasefire agreement" requires explicit public confirmation from both the US and Iranian governments that they have agreed to cease military hostilities against each other, or overwhelming consensus confirmed through media reports. US Vice President Vance previously reiterated that the deadline for Iran is 8 PM Eastern Time on Tuesday (8 AM Wednesday Beijing Time), and stated that Kharg Island does not signify a strategic shift. Earlier on Tuesday, two U.S. officials stated that the U.S. launched strikes on military targets on Kharg Island ahead of Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. One official said the U.S. struck more than 50 targets on the island. Officials said the strikes took place early Tuesday morning Eastern Time and did not target oil infrastructure. Trump had set Tuesday evening (Eastern Time) as the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, otherwise it would face the risk of devastating damage to its energy infrastructure. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing them in.

data.title: Fed's Williams: Tariffs Remain Key Part of Inflation Narrative
data.description: Federal Reserve official John Williams has emphasized the ongoing significance of tariffs in the inflation narrative. According to Jin10, Williams highlighted that tariffs continue to play a crucial role in shaping inflation dynamics, impacting various sectors of the economy. He noted that while other factors also contribute to inflationary pressures, tariffs remain a persistent element in the broader economic landscape. Williams' remarks underscore the complexity of inflation management and the multifaceted influences that policymakers must consider in their strategies.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams: Monetary policy can be adjusted if necessary, but for now, it's best to wait and see.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Williams stated that monetary policy could be adjusted if necessary, but for now, a wait-and-see approach is advisable. He projects GDP growth of 2% to 2.5% this year, with the unemployment rate remaining stable. He noted the labor market situation is quite complex and expects core inflation to decline later this year. (Jinshi)
